articles.description: Sikhulekelani Moyo/ Raymond Jaravaza ZIMBABWE yesterday joined the rest of the world in commemorating World Press Freedom Day, with the Government highlighting a significant expansion in the country’s media landscape...

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: DA criticises Ramaphosa over visit to Mnangagwa in Zimbabwe amid constitutional tensions
more.description: South Africa’s Democratic Alliance has condemned President Cyril Ramaphosa’s recent visit to Zimbabwean President Emmerson Mnangagwa, arguing it signals continued ANC support for authoritarian leadership in the region. The party also raised concerns about Zimbabwe’s proposed constitutional reforms and the treatment of opposition figures. The post DA criticises Ramaphosa over visit to Mnangagwa in Zimbabwe amid constitutional tensions appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: “Flawed and inadequate” – Teachers Union rejects mandatory ZIMSEC Exams plan
more.description: THE Amalgamated Rural Teachers Union of Zimbabwe (ARTUZ) has condemned government’s plan to make ZIMSEC examinations mandatory from 2027 as “inadequate and structurally flawed”. This follows a recent directive by Education Minister, Torerayi Moyo. Schools are, however, still allowed to offer the internationally recognized Cambridge examinations, but will have to apply for permission and demonstrate [...] The post “Flawed and inadequate” – Teachers Union rejects mandatory ZIMSEC Exams plan appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ZICOMO challenges Attorney General for warning VPs and Ministers who oppose Cabinet decisions
more.description: ZICOMO has criticised Attorney General Virginia Mabhiza’s reported warning against public dissent in Cabinet, arguing that constitutional freedoms remain paramount. The post ZICOMO challenges Attorney General for warning VPs and Ministers who oppose Cabinet decisions appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: 14 killed after Malawi bound bus overturns on Harare–Nyamapanda road
more.description: At least 14 people were killed when a long-distance luxury coach overturned along the Harare–Nyamapanda Road near Suswe on Tuesday afternoon, police have confirmed. The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) said the crash occurred at the 176km peg at around 1PM, involving a Malawi-bound BRD Luxury Coach travelling from Cape Town, South Africa. Police spokesperson Paul [...] The post 14 killed after Malawi bound bus overturns on Harare–Nyamapanda road appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Zimbabwe’s coup fears rock SADC as Cyril Ramaphosa comes to give President Mnangagwa 2 strong warnings
more.description: Zimbabwe’s Deepening Crisis: Ramaphosa’s Urgent Intervention Amidst Coup Fears and Constitutional Strife Harare – A palpable tension hangs over Zimbabwe as South African President Cyril Ramaphosa made an urgent, unannounced visit to President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s private Precabe Farm in Kwekwe. This clandestine meeting, shrouded in official ambiguity, has ignited a firestorm of speculation and concern [...] The post Zimbabwe’s coup fears rock SADC as Cyril Ramaphosa comes to give President Mnangagwa 2 strong warnings first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: 14 People Killed, Dozens Injured in Bus Accident Along Harare–Nyamapanda Highway
more.description: 14 People Killed, Dozens Injured in Bus Accident Along Harare–Nyamapanda Highway 14 people died on Tuesday after a long-distance luxury bus they were travelling in was involved in a crash along the Harare–Nyamapanda Highway. The fatal crash occurred on Tuesday afternoon near Suswe, leaving dozens injured and prompting an urgent response from emergency services. more.title: Byo residents call for end to water shedding
more.description: Source: Byo residents call for end to water shedding — CITEZW Bulawayo residents say they are hopeful that water supplies will improve after a strong rainy season boosted levels in the city’s dams. The residents expressed optimism that the Bulawayo City Council (BCC) could ease or end the long-running water rationing system, which has been [...] The post Byo residents call for end to water shedding appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZAPU claims constitutional changes could curb freedoms
more.description: ZAPU has accused the Zanu PF-led government of becoming “aggressive and dangerous” in the wake of the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CA3), warning that the country’s political environment is increasingly repressive and intolerant of dissent.

more.title: Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland
more.description: Source: Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland ⋆ Pindula News Monavale Wetland, or Monavale Vlei, in Harare, is one of only seven Wetlands of International Importance in Zimbabwe. It is now under threat, as 23 Members of Parliament have allegedly been allocated stands on the fragile ecosystem. According to [...] The post Harare’s Water Source At Risk As MPs Allocated Stands On Monavale Wetland appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it?
more.description: Source: Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it? -Newsday Zimbabwe Famine Early Warning Systems Network ZIMBABWE’S 2026 harvest is improving food availability after a difficult lean season, but rising prices, low incomes and below-average yields in some areas mean many households may still struggle to afford food, according to a new report by [...] The post Food is back — but can Zimbabweans afford it? appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZiG’s Future Depends On Purchasing Power, Not Paper Quality, Says Economist
more.description: Economist Luxon Zembe has said that long-term confidence in the ZiG will depend on the currency’s ability to retain value, rather than on the recent launch of better-quality banknotes.
